items.headline: Planning Commission to hear overview of state housing law SB 79
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission will hear a presentation on California's Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act (SB 79 from 2025). The law affects how local jurisdictions can approve housing near transit.

items.headline: Six-unit apartment building permitted on North 6th Street
items.summary: Alden Ventures received a permit to build a 6-unit apartment complex at 445 N 6th St for $879,706. This is the only new multi-family residential project in this batch, adding rental units in downtown San José rather than single-unit housing.

items.headline: Santa Clara Tourism District renewed after majority protest process
items.summary: City Council held a public hearing to renew the Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District following a majority protest proceeding and approved an agreement with Discover Santa Clara to administer the district. The district funds tourism marketing and promotion in Santa Clara.

items.headline: Council to adopt park dedication ordinance for Measure E site
items.summary: City Council is scheduled to recommend adoption of an ordinance dedicating a portion of the Measure E site as parkland. The item is a study session and staff presentation on the park dedication proposal.

items.headline: Financial feasibility study update on Baylands Golf Links driving range
items.summary: Council will review a financial feasibility study update for expanding the driving range at Baylands Golf Links. The study session will examine whether the expansion project makes financial sense for the city.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on an update to Cupertino's Housing Element on Tuesday, June 23, 2026. The Housing Element is the local plan that sets housing production targets and identifies land for residential development.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on June 22 to examine Sunnyvale's artificial turf policy. No decision or outcome is documented in the agenda data.

items.headline: Council to review public art in 762 San Antonio Road development
items.summary: The Public Art Commission reviewed public art requirements for the 762 San Antonio Road development project, with a presentation by the developer. This is part of the approval process for art integration in the private development.
